perturbation.original_text: 1.2 It is agreed that such appointment is nonexclusive and PPG SHANGHAI may, without obligation to pay DISTRIBUTOR any commission or other compensation, make direct sales of Products to other customers in the Territory and/or appoint additional distributors of Products in the Territory. During the term of this Agreement, DISTRIBUTOR shall have the exclusive right for selling the Products to Beijing Sino-US Jinche Yingang Auto Technological Services Limited (the "Auto Center"). PPG Shanghai or any of its direct or indirect affiliates shall not sell any of its products directly to the Auto Center or to any of the Auto Center's affiliates in China or throughout the world, unless PPG Shanghai obtains Distributor's written approval. It is understood and agreed that as used herein the term "PPG" shall mean PPG Industries Inc. and/or one or more affiliated companies of PPG Industries Inc. as the context may require.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 It is agreed that such appointment is nonexclusive and PPG SHANGHAI may, without obligation to pay DISTRIBUTOR any commission or other compensation, make direct sales of Products to other customers in the Territory and/or appoint additional distributors of Products in the Territory. During the term of this Agreement, DISTRIBUTOR may or may not have the exclusive right for selling the Products to Beijing Sino-US Jinche Yingang Auto Technological Services Limited (the "Auto Center"), depending on PPG Shanghai's discretion. PPG Shanghai or any of its direct or indirect affiliates may sell any of its products directly to the Auto Center or to any of the Auto Center's affiliates in China or throughout the world, regardless of Distributor's written approval. It is understood and agreed that as used herein the term "PPG" shall mean PPG Industries Inc. and/or one or more affiliated companies of PPG Industries Inc. as the context may require.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the exclusive right of the distributor to sell products to the 'Auto Center.' The original text grants exclusive rights but the changed text removes this certainty by stating 'DISTRIBUTOR may or may not have the exclusive right,' which contradicts the original definite statement. This creates uncertainty and potential disputes over sales to the Auto Center. Also, it has been added that 'PPG Shanghai or any of its direct or indirect affiliates may sell any of its products directly to the Auto Center or to any of the Auto Center's affiliates in China or throughout the world, regardless of Distributor's written approval' which is a contradiction to the original statement that states 'PPG Shanghai obtains Distributor's written approval'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 It is agreed that such appointment is nonexclusive and PPG SHANGHAI may, without obligation to pay DISTRIBUTOR any commission or other compensation, make direct sales of Products to other customers in the Territory and/or appoint additional distributors of Products in the Territory. During the term of this Agreement, DISTRIBUTOR may or may not have the exclusive right for selling the Products to Beijing Sino-US Jinche Yingang Auto Technological Services Limited (the "Auto Center"), depending on PPG Shanghai's discretion. PPG Shanghai or any of its direct or indirect affiliates may sell any of its products directly to the Auto Center or to any of the Auto Center's affiliates in China or throughout the world, regardless of Distributor's written approval. It is understood and agreed that as used herein the term "PPG" shall mean PPG Industries Inc. and/or one or more affiliated companies of PPG Industries Inc. as the context may require.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text grants DISTRIBUTOR an exclusive right to sell to 'Auto Center,' while the changed text makes this right discretionary ('may or may not have the exclusive right, depending on PPG Shanghai's discretion'). Additionally, the changed text allows PPG Shanghai to sell directly to the Auto Center without Distributor's approval, directly contradicting the original requirement for PPG Shanghai to obtain Distributor's written approval. Since the Changed Text is the same as the Contradicted text, there is indeed an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 PPG SHANGHAI agrees that PPG SHANGHAI will sell and DISTRIBUTOR agrees to buy the Products at the prices agreed to by the parties from time to time and subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. The prices in effect as of the Effective Date of the Agreement are set forth on Appendix 5. The prices for the Products must be agreed upon by PPG SHANGHAI at the time of PPG SHANGHAI's written acceptance of an order hereunder. Unless otherwise agreed by PPG SHANGHAI in writing, DISTRIBUTOR shall prepay all the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, and such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall only have the obligation to deliver the Products upon full and due payment.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 PPG SHANGHAI agrees that PPG SHANGHAI will sell and DISTRIBUTOR agrees to buy the Products at the prices solely determined by PPG SHANGHAI at its discretion and subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. The prices in effect as of the Effective Date of the Agreement as set forth on Appendix 5 are purely indicative and non-binding. The prices for the Products do not require agreement by PPG SHANGHAI at the time of PPG SHANGHAI's written acceptance of an order hereunder. DISTRIBUTOR may or may not be required to prepay all the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai, depending on PPG SHANGHAI's internal policies. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, regardless of whether such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall have the obligation to deliver the Products at any time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that prices are 'agreed to by the parties from time to time' and that 'prices for the Products must be agreed upon by PPG SHANGHAI'. The changed text contradicts this by stating prices are 'solely determined by PPG SHANGHAI at its discretion,' thus removing the element of mutual agreement. This change also creates uncertainty regarding the distributor's payment obligation, making it contradictory to the original statement. This can lead to disputes over pricing and payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 PPG SHANGHAI agrees that PPG SHANGHAI will sell and DISTRIBUTOR agrees to buy the Products at the prices solely determined by PPG SHANGHAI at its discretion and subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. The prices in effect as of the Effective Date of the Agreement as set forth on Appendix 5 are purely indicative and non-binding. The prices for the Products do not require agreement by PPG SHANGHAI at the time of PPG SHANGHAI's written acceptance of an order hereunder. DISTRIBUTOR may or may not be required to prepay all the invoiced amount in the currency and manner as indicated by PPG Shanghai, depending on PPG SHANGHAI's internal policies. DISTRIBUTOR shall be deemed to complete its payment obligation upon full payment of the invoiced sum, regardless of whether such amount has been allocated into the account as directed by PPG SHANGHAI. PPG SHANGHAI shall have the obligation to deliver the Products at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates mutually agreed-upon pricing, whereas the changed text grants PPG SHANGHAI sole discretion over pricing, directly contradicting the initial agreement and creating a strong conflict. Furthermore, the original text specifies that prepayment is required, while the changed text makes it optional, subject to PPG SHANGHAI's internal policies. This directly contradicts the original mandatory prepayment clause. Therefore, the contradiction exists because the changed text introduces elements of unilateral control and optional payment terms, which are fundamentally incompatible with the original agreement's bilateral pricing and mandatory prepayment structure.

perturbation.original_text: 18.2 The parties hereof intend to form a long-term relationship. To this end, if both parties wish to renew this Agreement, the parties shall agree on such intention in writing at least thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement. The parties shall agree on the terms and conditions of the renewal, and enter into a new agreement within sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement. During this sixty(60) days period, both parties shall continue to perform their respective obligation under the same terms and conditions of this Agreement. 18.3 In the event that there is no written notice issued by either party to show the intention to renew this Agreement in the said thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement, the Agreement shall terminate at the end of the said thirty(30) days. In the event that such a notice of intention to renew is issued, but the parties are not able to enter into a new agreement within that sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement shall terminate at the end of the said sixty(60) days.
perturbation.changed_text: 18.2 The parties hereof may or may not intend to form a long-term relationship, at PPG Shanghai's sole discretion. To this end, if PPG Shanghai wishes to renew this Agreement, the distributor is automatically obligated to agree on such intention in writing at least thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement. The parties shall adhere to the terms and conditions of the renewal, as dictated by PPG Shanghai, and may or may not enter into a new agreement within sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement. During this sixty(60) days period, only PPG Shanghai shall continue to perform their respective obligation under any terms and conditions it sees fit, without the distributor. 18.3 In the event that there is no written notice issued by PPG Shanghai to show the intention to renew this Agreement in the said thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement, the Agreement may or may not terminate at the end of the said thirty(30) days, as per the discretion of PPG Shanghai. In the event that such a notice of intention to renew is issued, but the parties are not able to enter into a new agreement within that sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement may or may not terminate at the end of the said sixty(60) days, as per the discretion of PPG Shanghai.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly indicates the intentions of both parties to have a long-term relationship and renew the contract through mutual written agreement. However, in the altered version, it states 'The parties hereof may or may not intend to form a long-term relationship, at PPG Shanghai's sole discretion', making the first intention of both parties no longer clear. Also the statement 'PPG Shanghai wishes to renew this Agreement, the distributor is automatically obligated to agree' makes the contract no longer have mutual intention and agreement between the parties. This contradicts the intent for both parties to agree on the terms and conditions
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18.2 The parties hereof may or may not intend to form a long-term relationship, at PPG Shanghai's sole discretion. To this end, if PPG Shanghai wishes to renew this Agreement, the distributor is automatically obligated to agree on such intention in writing at least thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement. The parties shall adhere to the terms and conditions of the renewal, as dictated by PPG Shanghai, and may or may not enter into a new agreement within sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement. During this sixty(60) days period, only PPG Shanghai shall continue to perform their respective obligation under any terms and conditions it sees fit, without the distributor. 18.3 In the event that there is no written notice issued by PPG Shanghai to show the intention to renew this Agreement in the said thirty(30) days before the expiry of the current Term of the Agreement, the Agreement may or may not terminate at the end of the said thirty(30) days, as per the discretion of PPG Shanghai. In the event that such a notice of intention to renew is issued, but the parties are not able to enter into a new agreement within that sixty(60) days from the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement may or may not terminate at the end of the said sixty(60) days, as per the discretion of PPG Shanghai.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 18.2 introduces a strong contradiction. The original text establishes a mutual intention for a long-term relationship and renewal through mutual agreement. However, the modified version grants PPG Shanghai sole discretion over the intention to renew and obligates the distributor to agree. This directly contradicts the principle of mutual agreement and creates an imbalance of power, rendering the original intention of a long-term relationship and mutual renewal process unenforceable. Further, the changed text states that PPG Shanghai can dictate the terms and conditions of the renewal, and may or may not enter into a new agreement, directly conflicting with any prior understanding of a mutually agreed upon contract. The introduction of 'may or may not' for termination also creates direct conflict and uncertainty.
